blocks.statement: I can decompose a launch velocity into vₓ = v cos θ and v_y = v sin θ, compute flight time, and explain why 45° maximizes range on level ground.

blocks.whatWeKnow: 2026-XJ's velocity has both horizontal and angled components in Earth's frame.
blocks.connection: If the asteroid entered the atmosphere, this is the math we'd use to predict the impact point.

blocks.markdown:
## Angled launches — split the velocity first

An angled launch is just a horizontal launch wearing a disguise. Strip it with two lines of trig:

- **vₓ = v·cos θ** — the horizontal piece (constant the whole flight)
- **v_y = v·sin θ** — the vertical piece (gravity eats it at g per second)

On level ground the flight time is **t = 2·v_y/g** (up and back down), and the range is **x = vₓ·t**.

Why is **45°** the max-range angle on level ground? Because the speed is split **equally** between the two jobs: enough v_y to buy time in the air, enough vₓ to use that time covering ground. Tilt steeper and you buy time you can't use; tilt shallower and you have speed with no time to spend it.

blocks.prompt: A ball launches at 20 m/s at 30° above level ground. Find (a) vₓ, (b) v_y, (c) flight time, (d) range.
blocks.given: v = 20 m/s · θ = 30° · level ground · g = 9.81 m/s²
blocks.equation: vₓ = v cos θ · v_y = v sin θ · t_flight = 2·v_y/g · x = vₓ·t_flight
blocks.answer: ≈ 35 m. (Try 45° — you'll get ≈ 40.8 m, the maximum on level ground.)

blocks.instruction: Pick a launch speed v. Draw three arcs from the same launch point: one at 30°, one at 45°, one at 60°. Make the 45° arc go farthest. Then write a 1-sentence reason WHY 45° wins.
prompts: Same launch speed for all three arcs.
30° and 60° should land at about the SAME spot (try it tomorrow with the math!).
Your one-sentence WHY in a corner.

blocks.prompt: Same launch speed (20 m/s), but now θ = 60°. Predict first: will the range be bigger, smaller, or the same as 30°? Then compute it.
blocks.givenHint: v = 20 m/s, θ = 60°, level ground. Write your prediction in the Given box too.
blocks.equationHint: Same four-equation chain as the worked example: components → flight time → range.

blocks.prompt: Why does 45° give max range on level ground? Reason it physically (not just algebraically).
blocks.frame: On level ground, 45° gives max range because the launch speed is split ___ between horizontal and vertical, balancing ___ in the air with ___ across the ground.
